title: astragalus gandomanicus
intro: Astragalus gandomanicus, also known as the Gandomani milkvetch, is a species of flowering plant in the legume family, Fabaceae. It is a perennial herb native to Iran, and its roots have been traditionally used for medicinal purposes due to their rich content of flavonoids, alkaloids, and other bioactive compounds. The medicinal properties of Astragalus gandomanicus are believed to have anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and immunomodulatory effects, which can help to alleviate various health issues such as fever, respiratory infections, and gastrointestinal disorders. Astragalus gandomanicus is often prepared as a herbal tea or decoction, and it is also used in traditional Iranian medicine as a tonic and adaptogen to promote overall health and well-being.
